title: quercus faginea subsp. baetica
intro: Quercus faginea subsp. baetica, also known as the Baetic oak or the Iberian oak, is a subspecies of oak tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The medicinal properties of Quercus faginea subsp. baetica lie in its bark, leaves, and acorns, which are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Quercus faginea subsp. baetica include its potential to reduce cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus faginea subsp. baetica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues and skin problems.
